upon your personal situation, may also carry out some other tests.
In this booklet, several situations are described where you should stop using Yasmin, or where
reliability of Yasmin may be decreased. In such situations you should either not have sex or you shoul
take extra non-hormonal contraceptive precautions, e.g. use a condom or another barrier method. Do no
changes of body temperature and cervical mucus.
particularly important to read the symptoms of a blood clot – see section 2 “Blood clots”.
and that of your close relatives. The doctor will also measure your blood
upon your personal situation, may also carry out some other tests.
In this booklet, several situations are described where you should stop using Yasmin, or where
reliability of Yasmin may be decreased. In such situations you should either not have sex or you shoul
take extra non-hormonal contraceptive precautions, e.g. use a condom or another barrier method. Do no
changes of body temperature and cervical mucus.
You should not use Yasmin if you have any of the conditions listed below. If you do have any of t
birth control would be more appropriate.
your lungs (pulmonary embolus, PE) or other organs;
first sign of a heart attack) or transient ischaemic attack (TIA – temporary stroke symptoms);
(listed in section 6). This may cause itching, rash or swelling.

clot in the leg (i.e. deep vein thrombosis), a blood clot in the lung (i.e. pulmonar
embolism), a heart attack or a stroke (see ‘Blood clots’ section below).
clot in the leg (i.e. deep vein thrombosis), a blood clot in the lung (i.e. pulmonar
embolism), a heart attack or a stroke (see ‘Blood clots’ section below).

condition develops, or gets worse while you are using Yasmin, you should also tell your doctor.
this condition. Hypertriglyceridaemia has been associated with an increased risk of developi
how soon after delivery you can start taking Yasmin;
if you have a disease that first appeared during pregnancy or earlier use of sex hormones (
known as “pregnancy patches”). If so, avoid direct sunlight or ultraviolet light.
If you experience symptoms of angioedema such as swollen face, tongue and/or throat and

HOW TO RECOGNISE A BLOOD CLOT
If you are unsure, talk to a doctor as some of these symptom

immediate and full recovery, but you should still seek urgen
medical attention as you may be at risk of another stroke.
The use of combined hormonal contraceptives has been connected with an increase in the risk
they occur in the first year of use of a combined hormonal contraceptive.
The risk of developing a blood clot in a vein is highest during the first year of taking a comb
hormonal contraceptive (the same product or a different product) after a break of 4 weeks or more.
pregnant, about 2 will develop a blood clot in a year.

(e.g. below the age of about 50). In this case you could have a hereditary blood clotting disorder;
illness, or you have your leg in a cast. The use of Yasmin may need to be stopped several week
doctor may decide that Yasmin needs to be stopped.

. When using a combined hormonal contraceptive like Yasmin, you are advised to s
50). In this case you could also have a higher risk of having a heart attack or stroke;
developing a blood clot may be increased even more.
family member experiences a thrombosis for no known reason; or you gain a lot of weight, tell you
Breast cancer has been observed slightly more often in women using combination pills, but it is n
women on combination pills because they are examined by their doctor more often. The occurence
breast tumours becomes gradually less after stopping the combination hormonal contraceptives. It
important to regularly check your breasts and you should contact your doctor if you feel any lump.
in pill users. Contact your doctor if you have unusually severe abdominal pain.
Some women using hormonal contraceptives including Yasmin have reported depression or depress
changes and depressive symptoms contact your doctor for further medical advice as soon as possible.
During the first few months that you are taking Yasmin, you may have unexpected bleeding (bleed
your doctor must find out what is wrong.
taken any other medicines, it is highly unlikely that you are pregnant.
immediately. Do not start the next strip until you are sure that you are not pregnant.
Always tell your doctor which medicines or herbal products you are already using. Also tell any ot
you if you need to take additional contraceptive precautions (for example condoms) and if so, for h
long, or, whether the use of another medicine you need must be changed.
Always tell your doctor which medicines or herbal products you are already using. Also tell any ot
you if you need to take additional contraceptive precautions (for example condoms) and if so, for h
long, or, whether the use of another medicine you need must be changed.
transcriptase inhibitors, such as ritonavir, nevirapine, efavirenz)
Do not use Yasmin if you have hepatitis C and are taking the medicinal products containi
sofosbuvir/velpatasvir/voxilaprevir, as these products may cause increases in liver function blood te
results (increase in ALT liver enzyme). Your doctor will prescribe another type of contraceptive prior
completion of this treatment. See section “Do not use Yasmin”.
hormone contraceptives can affect the results of some tests.
If you are pregnant, you must not take Yasmin. If you become pregnant while taking Yasmin you m
any time (see also page 15 “If you stop taking Yasmin”).
while you are breast-feeding you should contact your doctor.
with or without food, but you should take the tablets every day around the same time.
be taken. If, for example you start on a Wednesday, take a tablet with “WED” next to it. Follow
direction of the arrow on the strip until all 21 tablets have been taken.
Then take no tablets for 7 days. In the course of these 7
tablet-free days (otherwise called a stop or
On the 8th day after the last Yasmin tablet (that is, after the 7-day gap week), you should start with
the same day of the week
and that the withdrawal bleed should occur on the same days each month.
Begin with Yasmin on the first day of the cycle (that is, the first day of your period). If you sta
You can start Yasmin preferably on
previous pill (or after the last inactive tablet of your previous pill). When changing from
combination contraceptive vaginal ring or patch, follow the advice of your doctor.
removal, from an injectable when the next injection would be due) but in all of these cases use ext
protective measures (for example, a condom) for the first 7 days of tablet-taking.
pregnant or wait until your next period.
Even girls who have not yet started to menstruate but have accidentally taken this medicine
late taking a tablet, the protection against pregnancy is not reduc
The greater the number of tablets that you have forgotten, the greater is the risk of beco
the end of the strip. Therefore, you should keep to the following rules (see the diagram on page 14):
the same time. Continue taking the tablets at the usual time and use
days, for example, a condom. If you have had sex in the week before forgetting the tablet you may
pregnant. In that case, contact your doctor.
the same time. Continue taking the tablets at the usual time. The protection against pregnancy is no
reduced, and you do not need to take extra precautions.
tablets at the same time. Continue taking the tablets at the usual time. Instead of having s
pill-free days start the next strip.
menstruation-like bleeding during the second strip.
You can also stop the strip and go directly to the tablet-free period of 7 days
tablet-free period, you may be pregnant. Contact your doctor before you start the next strip.
If you vomit within 3-4 hours after taking a tablet or you have severe diarrhoea, there is a risk that th
active substances in the pill will not be fully taken up by your body. The situation is almost the same
forgetting a tablet. After vomiting or diarrhoea, take another tablet from a reserve strip as soon
12 hours have passed, you should follow
the advice given under “If you forget to take Yasmin” on pa
instead of the tablet-free period and finishing it. You may experience light or menstruation-like bleedi
while using this second strip. After the usual tablet-free period of 7 days,
If you take the tablets according to the instructions, then your period will begin
– 7 is the maximum!). For example, if your tablet-free days normally begin on a Friday, and you
the tablet-free interval very short (for example, 3 days or less) you may not have any bleeding du
these days. You may then experience light or menstruation-like bleeding.
for advice about other reliable methods of birth control. If you want to become pregnant, stop takin
period before trying to become pregnant. You will be able to calculate th
side effect, particularly if severe and persistent, or have any change to your health that you think may b
due to Yasmin, please talk to your doctor.
arteries (arterial thromboembolism (ATE)) is present for all women taking combined hormo
contraceptives. For more detailed information on the different risks from taking combined hormona
contraceptives please see section 2 “What you need to know before you take Yasmin”.
multiforme (characterized by rash with target-shaped reddening or sores).
The chance of having a blood clot may be higher if you have any other conditions that increase this ris
If you get any side effects, talk to your doctor or pharmacist.
This includes any possible side effects
you can help provide more information on the safety of this medicine.
“EXP”. The expiry date refers to the last day of that month.
away medicines you no longer use. These measures will help protect the environment.

round with convex surfaces, one side is marked with the letters "DO" in a regular hexagon.
